contractSurface.securityClassification: engine-internal bootstrap, GraphRAG seeding, family implementation loop, five-arbiter consensus, regression impact, and context-pack assembly
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-33 uses system-initiation-bootstrap as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug.
coreGuarantees: Bootstrap sentinel state is read before every phase.
Bootstrap phases execute in order and cannot be skipped.
Dry-run execution validates without AI calls or state mutation.
Plan bundle import is all-or-nothing and rolls back on partial failure.
GraphRAG Layer 2 starts only after Layer 1 is verified complete.
Family implementation retries are bounded by FREEDOM configuration.
Five arbiters run in parallel and require at least four passing verdicts.
Regression impact analysis runs before any promotion.
Context packs are TTL-managed and stale packs are refreshed before reuse.
Evolved prompts apply only to new sessions, never in-flight generation.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Tenant scope remains automatic through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

machineLockedRules: sentinel state read before any phase
seeding batches are idempotent by node ID
implementation status uses a composite idempotency key
state transitions persist before emitting events
retry count comes from FREEDOM config
max retry overflow escalates to human review
evolved prompts never affect in-flight generation
all five arbiters run in parallel
quorum threshold remains at least four of five
arbiter prompts stay seeded in the T540 genesis prompt
regression impact check runs before promotion
active bundle manifests are scanned after promotion cases that can affect consumers
context pack TTL comes from FREEDOM config
stale context packs are refreshed before reuse
hybrid RAG output is merged with database metadata

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-33 values may tune module naming, timeout budgets, GraphRAG batch size, retry budget, context-pack freshness, RAG result count, graph depth, status-retention windows, dry-run availability, and arbiter timeout without changing bootstrap ordering, tenant isolation, fabric boundaries, quorum semantics, or regression-gate behavior.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to internal engine bootstrap naming, operational time budgets, RAG and graph retrieval sizing, retry and timeout budgets, status-retention windows, and dry-run availability.

edgeCases.name: Acme bootstrap control room
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes the bootstrap package for a strict platform-administrator control room with short context freshness and faster arbiter timeouts.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module naming, phase timeout, context-pack TTL, arbiter timeout
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Ordering, tenant isolation, fabric access, parallel arbiter quorum, and regression gate semantics remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind self-build workbench
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the package for a partner self-build workbench with larger context windows, more generation retries, and wider graph traversal.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: retry budget, RAG result count, graph depth, status TTL
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Generation remains bounded, stale packs remain forbidden, and promotions remain regression-gated.

edgeCases.name: Tessera initiation studio
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes the package for a community initiation studio with slower status expiration, smaller GraphRAG batches, and longer human-review-friendly timeouts.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: batch size, phase timeout, status TTL, context-pack TTL
edgeCases.currentImplementation: No tenant can relax DNA, BFA, tenant-scope, outbox, CloudEvents, or quorum rules.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T536 through T542 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1339 through F1348 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Bootstrap sentinel state is read before every phase.
Dry-run mode has zero AI calls and zero state mutation.
GraphRAG Layer 2 starts only after Layer 1 is complete.
Implementation status uses a composite idempotency key.
Five arbiters execute in parallel and require at least four passing verdicts.
Regression impact analysis runs before promotion.
Stale context packs are refreshed before reuse.
Tenant scope is resolved through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

tenantABootstrapControlRoom.description: Tenant A tunes FLOW-33 for an internal control room with tighter operating windows.
tenantABootstrapControlRoom.impact: Acme gets a faster platform-admin bootstrap package while all bootstrap and consensus invariants stay unchanged.
tenantABootstrapControlRoom.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-33 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-33 package assembly copies the system-initiation-bootstrap implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
